scope_note: QA Witt Tower Recursive Refinement Law cert [433]. Closes the gap cert [432] explicitly states it does not cover: the exact classification of new periods at level p^3 and beyond. Primary sources: Wall, D.D. (1960) 'Fibonacci series modulo m' American Mathematical Monthly 67(6) doi:10.2307/2309169 (Pisano period tower); Serre, J.-P. (1979) 'Local Fields' doi:10.1007/978-1-4757-5673-9 SS II.4 (Witt vector tower structure); Ireland, K. and Rosen, M. (1990) 'A Classical Introduction to Modern Number Theory' ISBN 978-0-387-97329-6 Ch.7 (Lagrange's theorem on element orders, Hensel lifting). CLAIM (narrow and falsifiable): for sigma_m(a,b)=(a+b mod m, a mod m) (identical map to [389] and [432]): (C1) RECURSIVE_PERIOD_SET_LAW: Periods_nt(p^(k+1)) = Periods_nt(p^k) union p*Periods_nt(p^k) holds at EVERY transition k->k+1 (not just k=1->2 as [389] states), with old-part orbit counts exactly frozen -- verified for p in {7,11,13,17,19} at transitions (1,2) and (2,3). (C2) SPLIT_UNEQUAL_GENERAL_K_MULTIPLICITY: for split-unequal primes with eigenvalue orders ord_min<ord_max, count_new(p^(k-1)*ord_min)=(p-1)/ord_min (CONSTANT in k, not just k=2) and count_new(p^(k-1)*ord_max)=(p-1)*(p^k+p^(k-1)-1)/ord_max (generalizes [432]'s k=2-only formula (p-1)(p^2+p-1)/ord_max) -- verified by brute-force orbit decomposition for p in {11,19} at k in {2,3}, p in {29,31} at k=2, plus p=11 at k=4 (gathered during development, cited here, not re-run in automated self-test for runtime reasons). (C3) INERT_SPLIT_EQUAL_GENERAL_K_MULTIPLICITY: count_new(p^(k-1)*pi(p))=p^(k-1)*(p^2-1)/pi(p) (explicit closed form generalizing [432]'s C5 'p*count_old' corollary to all k) -- verified for p in {7,13,17,23} at k in {2,3}, plus p=7 at k in {4,5} (k=5 gathered during development, cited here). (C4) NONDEGENERACY_WIDE_SCAN: the non-degenerate Hensel-lift assumption underlying C1-C3 (multiplicative order grows by exactly one factor of p per tower level, no Wieferich-like stalling) checked via fast modular arithmetic (Hensel-lifted root order in (Z/p^kZ)* for split primes; Galois-ring GR(p^k,2) order for inert primes) -- 78 split primes <1000 at k=2..10 (702 checks) and 31 inert primes <300 at k=2..8 (217 checks), zero exceptions, run live inside the validator's self-test. DERIVATION: C2/C3 generalize [432]'s eigen-coordinate stratification argument inductively -- in the c2=0-exactly stratum every unit c1 shares the identical period p^(k-1)*ord_min regardless of value, so count=size/period=(p-1)/ord_min with the p^(k-1) factor cancelling exactly; the complementary stratum's size by total-count conservation gives the ord_max formula; the inert/split-equal case needs no stratification since there is only one new period per level, so conservation alone suffices. RELATION TO [432]/[389]: not a duplicate (checked by reading both in full before drafting) -- [389] gives the period SET at k=1->2 only; [432] proves the embedding mechanism unconditionally for all k and the multiplicity formula at k=2 only; this cert is the first to state and verify the closed-form multiplicity at EVERY k, plus the recursive period-set law at every transition (not just the first), plus an independent fast non-degeneracy check far wider than any brute-force orbit enumeration in the chain. THEOREM NT: sigma/Hensel-lift/Galois-ring arithmetic is pure integer mod p^k throughout; no float, no observer layer. 4 checks PASS; self-test ok (17.6s). Derived 2026-06-16.
